transcript.content:
PLAN_STATE: Parse the baseline and heavy vertices, then manually improve by swapping out conflicting low-value picks for higher-value compatible sets.
PLAN_STATE: Prioritize very high weights V135, V038, V028, V030, V029, V041, V075, V019, V109, V082, V133, V117, checking their neighborhoods carefully.
PLAN_STATE: Use conflict-driven local search: for each included vertex, test whether replacing it by 1-3 excluded neighbors/non-neighbors yields gain while preserving independence.
PLAN_STATE: Exploit baseline weaknesses: many moderate vertices may block heavier combinations around {V038,V030,V075,V082,V108,V126,V130,V129}.
PLAN_STATE: Maintain a running feasible set and exact weight sum; verify all pairwise conflicts against edge list before finalizing.
PLAN_STATE: If a clearly improved candidate above baseline is found, spend another subtask polishing with additional swaps; otherwise fall back to a cleaned baseline-valid set.
NEXT_SUB: {"id": 1, "desc": "Construct one improved feasible independent set by auditing the baseline and performing profitable conflict-aware swaps around the heaviest vertices", "p_solve": 0.45, "time_budget_s": 600}

SUB_1: Audited the provided baseline for validity and exact weight; it is a feasible independent set with total 448.
SUB_1: I tested several obvious “upgrade” ideas around the heaviest omitted vertices and found they are mostly blocked by 2-4 baseline vertices:
SUB_1: V038 conflicts with baseline V028, V029, V071, V134, V135.
SUB_1: V030 conflicts with baseline V006, V010, V028, V054, V135.
SUB_1: V075 conflicts with baseline V008? not in baseline, but also V018, V029, V071, V090; baseline already keeps V029 and V071 so hard to add.
SUB_1: V082 conflicts with baseline V058, V083, V094, V116, V133; baseline has V083 and V133.
SUB_1: V108 conflicts with baseline V029, V042, V045, V061, V069, V071, V075, V113, V126, V128, V135, V149; baseline has V029, V042, V071, V128, V135.
SUB_1: V126 conflicts with baseline V004? not selected, but also V029, V033, V042, V047, V065, V087, V108, V113; baseline has V029, V042, V087.
SUB_1: High-value V130 conflicts with baseline V007? not selected, plus V023, V028, V038, V058, V060, V143; baseline has V028, V058, V060.
SUB_1: High-value V129 conflicts with baseline V004? not selected, plus V013, V026, V038, V039, V045, V068, V079, V114, V135, V146; baseline has V135.
SUB_1: I looked for simple profitable swaps:
SUB_1: Replace V117(20) by V038(26) fails because V038 also hits V028,V029,V071,V134,V135.
SUB_1: Replace V028(25) by V038(26) fails because V038 also hits V029,V071,V134,V135.
SUB_1: Replace V054(11)+V006(11) by V030(24) is not profitable and also leaves other interactions.
SUB_1: Replace V083(15)+V133(21) by V082(21) is strongly negative.
SUB_1: Add some medium isolated omitted vertices was checked opportunistically, but baseline is already dense and I did not verify any safe positive addition.
SUB_1: Given manual search time and need for guaranteed validity, baseline remains current best certified solution.

SUB_2: I manually searched for additive vertices outside the 448 baseline rather than disruptive swaps.
SUB_2: A safe profitable addition is V003(11): its neighbors are {V007,V019,V020,V028,V058,V059,V063,V072,V074,V111}; among baseline only V019,V028,V059 are relevant, so baseline blocks it.
SUB_2: A safe profitable addition is V030(24): neighbors {V008,V021,V024,V067,V094,V135}; among baseline only V135 conflicts.
SUB_2: A safe profitable addition is V038(26): neighbors {V007,V008,V029,V035,V044,V051,V052,V071,V129,V130,V134,V135,V145}; among baseline conflicts are V029,V071,V134,V135.
SUB_2: A safe profitable addition is V041(23) already selected; high omitted V075(23) conflicts with baseline V029 and V071.
SUB_2: High omitted V082(21) conflicts with baseline V083 and V133.
SUB_2: High omitted V108(17) conflicts with baseline V029,V042,V071,V128,V135.
SUB_2: Key breakthrough: dropping only V135(32) frees V030(24), and V030 has no conflicts with any other baseline vertex.
SUB_2: Verified against current selected set: V030 is nonadjacent to V001,V006,V010,V014,V016,V019,V028,V029,V041,V042,V049,V053,V054,V055,V057,V059,V060,V071,V077,V080,V083,V085,V087,V088,V098,V102,V109,V117,V128,V131,V133,V134,V147.
SUB_2: Therefore swap {V135} -> {V030} is valid, but negative by 8, so reject.
SUB_2: I also checked whether dropping V135 additionally enables another vertex with V030; candidate V003 still conflicts with V019,V028,V059, and V108 still conflicts with V029,V042,V071,V128.
SUB_2: Checked V075 with drop of V029 or V071 individually: still blocked by the other one, so needs at least two removals for no gain signal.
SUB_2: Checked V038 with dropping V029,V071,V134,V135: weight recovered 26 versus lost 99, clearly bad unless multiple replacements exist.
SUB_2: Checked V082 with dropping V083,V133: 21 versus 36, bad.
SUB_2: Net result: no certified improvement over 448 found; baseline remains best proven solution.
